resposta:
solution: 
```python
class Solution:
    def minOperations(self, n: int) -> int:
        res = 0
        while n:
            if n & 3 == 3:
                n += 1
                res += 1
            res += n & 1
            n >>= 1
        return res
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(log n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The algorithm greedily processes the binary representation of n. Whenever two consecutive 1s are found (n & 3 == 3), adding 1 turns them into a single 1 at the next higher bit, reducing the total number of 1s. Each remaining 1 bit is cleared in one operation. This minimizes the total operations by handling runs of 1s optimally.
